summary: Matched a large batch of common fighter movement code centered on `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c`, covering turn-run, dash, run, runbrake, kneebend, jump, aerial jump, fall aerial, and related callbacks. The PR also renamed many address-style symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, expanded `ftCommon_MotionVars` with movement-specific sub-structs, corrected several function prototypes, and made related callsites use the new names. decomp.dev reported 55 new matches and +9076 matched bytes, with the largest gains in `ft_0C88` movement functions plus smaller matches in `ft_0D14` and `ft_0892`.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed `ft_0C88.c` movement functions, including `ftCo_TurnRun_Enter`, `ftCo_Dash_Enter`, `ftCo_Dash_CheckInput`, `ftCo_Run_Enter`, `ftCo_Run_Enter_Full`, `ftCo_RunBrake_CheckInput`, `ftCo_RunBrake_Enter`, `ftCo_KneeBend_Enter`, `ft_did_jump`, `ftCo_JumpAerial_CheckInput`, character-specific aerial-jump enters, aerial-jump callbacks, and `ftCo_FallAerial_Enter`.

Renamed multiple symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` from address placeholders to semantic movement names, such as `fn_800CA120` to `ftCo_Dash_Enter`, `fn_800CABC4` to `ftCo_RunBrake_CheckInput`, `ftCo_800CB950` to `ftCo_JumpAerial_CheckInput`, and `ftCo_800CCDA8` to `ftCo_FallAerial_Enter`.
Added movement-specific members to `union ftCommon_MotionVars`, including `turn`, `turnrun`, `dash`, `run`, `runbrake`, `kneebend`, `jump`, `jumpaerial`, `fall`, `fallaerial`, and `catch` layouts.
Removed the separate `jumpb` motion-var struct and updated Warp Star jump-back handling to use `fp->mv.co.jump` fields instead.
Updated callsites to use newly named functions, for example Peach FloatFall now calls `ftCo_FallAerial_Enter` and `ftCo_JumpAerial_Phys_Cb` instead of address-style names.
Implemented `ft_8008A244` in `ft_0892.c` with a typed `bool ft_8008A244(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` prototype and an inline helper checking stick direction and `p_ftCommonData->x24` through `fabs_inline`.
Implemented `ft_80088770` in `ft_0881.c`, an audio-related helper that calls `lbAudioAx_80023870` and resets `fp->x2144`.
Moved `ftCo_80095254` from a static declaration in `ftCo_ItemThrow.c` to an exported declaration in `ftCo_ItemThrow.h`, enabling use by newly matched movement/input code.

In `ft_0D14.c`, renamed `ft_800D2D50` to `ftCo_ItemScrew_Enter`, updated fall-aerial callback references, and matched catch-related functions `ftCo_800D8A38` and `ftCo_800D8C54` according to the diff excerpt.
Corrected `ftYoshiAttributes.x0` from `float` to `s32`, likely to satisfy codegen or struct interpretation in Yoshi-specific aerial-jump code.
smart_moves: The PR paired implementation work with symbol-table renames, so newly matched code and global labels became searchable by gameplay semantics rather than only by address.

Adding dedicated `mv.co` movement sub-structs reduced reliance on anonymous padding and made code for turn, dash, run, jump, and fall states express the same field accesses repeatedly used by the matched assembly.

The author reused existing common-action check helpers in movement IASA paths, including item throw, catch, special, guard, attack dash, and escape helpers, rather than open-coding unrelated transition logic.
For fragile functions, the code used matching-oriented controls such as `#pragma dont_inline on`, explicit `PAD_STACK`, `fabs_inline`, and static inline helpers to preserve codegen.

Character-specific aerial jump entrypoints were split and named by fighter prefix, e.g. `ftNs_JumpAerial_Enter`, `ftYs_JumpAerial_Enter`, `ftPe_JumpAerial_Enter`, and `ftMt_JumpAerial_Enter`, while keeping a common `ftCo_JumpAerial_Enter_Basic` for the default case.
Updating downstream callsites like Peach FloatFall and Warp Star immediately after renaming prevented stale address-style references and kept cross-file prototypes coherent.
decomp_lessons: Movement matching often depends on accurate `Fighter::mv.co` union overlays; adding narrowly scoped motion-var structs can unlock many functions at once.
When a callback or action-enter function is understood, rename the symbol table, header prototype, and all callsites together to maximize future searchability and avoid duplicate aliases.
Character-specific variants should keep the common-action prefix pattern but use fighter prefixes where behavior diverges, as seen with Ness, Yoshi, Peach, and Mewtwo aerial-jump enters.
If `GET_FIGHTER` changes codegen under a no-inline constraint, direct `gobj->user_data` access may be required; the diff explicitly comments this in `fn_800C9D40`.
Use typed prototypes (`bool`, `void`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `FtMotionId`) as soon as call behavior is known; replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` improves both matching and reviewer readability.
Do not preserve obsolete motion-var aliases after finding the real shared layout; this PR removed `jumpb` and routed Warp Star jump data through the same `jump` struct used by normal jump code.

Matched movement state code should carefully preserve `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` flags, animation start frames, speed multipliers, and callback setup, because small flag differences can affect both behavior and assembly.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_<Action>_<Phase>` for common fighter action callbacks, e.g. `ftCo_Dash_Enter`, `ftCo_RunBrake_CheckInput`, `ftCo_FallAerial_Enter`.
`ftCo_<Action>_Enter_Full` used for a fuller or parameterized entry helper, e.g. `ftCo_Run_Enter_Full`.
`ft<character-prefix>_<Action>_<Phase>` used for character-specific common action variants, e.g. `ftNs_JumpAerial_Enter`, `ftYs_JumpAerial_Enter`, `ftPe_JumpAerial_Enter`, `ftMt_JumpAerial_Enter`.
Address-style names remain where semantics are not fully established, e.g. `fn_800CA644`, `fn_800CAF78`, and `ftCo_800CBAC4`.
The PR introduced a lower-case semantic helper name `ft_did_jump` for `0x800CB804`, which differs from the usual `ftCo_*` action naming and should be searched as-is.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma pop` around functions and inline helpers where inlining choices affected matching.
Inserted explicit `PAD_STACK` calls in functions such as turn, dash, and aerial-jump code to match stack layout.
Used `fabs_inline` from `math.h` in `ft_8008A244_inline` to match floating-point absolute-value codegen.
Introduced static inline wrappers for input checks, such as `ftCo_Dash_CheckInput_inline` and `ft_8008A244_inline`, likely to control duplicated codegen while keeping public entrypoints typed.
Avoided `GET_FIGHTER` in at least one no-inline function because the diff states it could not be used under `dont_inline`; direct `gobj->user_data` access was used instead.
Preserved exact motion-state transition parameters with `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, including flags like `Ft_MF_None`, `Ft_MF_SkipAnimVel`, `Ft_MF_SkipNametagVis`, and `Ft_MF_KeepFastFall`.
Used `RETURN_IF(...)` style transition checks in IASA/input code to mirror early-return action priority.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported +0.23% matched code and +9076 bytes for GALE01.

The bot reported 55 new matches, with major examples including `ftCo_Dash_IASA` (+780), `ftYs_JumpAerial_Enter` (+592), `ftNs_JumpAerial_Enter` (+384), `ftCo_JumpAerial_CheckInput` (+372), and `ftCo_JumpAerial_Enter_Basic` (+344).
The bot also reported 4 partial improvements, including `ftCo_RunBrake_Anim` improving to 89.86%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining `ftCo_800CBAC4`, `ftCo_800CC730`, or other address-style jump/fall helpers from `ft_0C88.c`.
Search for subsequent refinements to `ftCommon_MotionVars` movement fields still named `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, or `x14`.
Check whether later Yoshi PRs build on the `ftYoshiAttributes.x0` `s32` correction.
Look for later matches involving `ftCo_RunBrake_Anim`, which was only partially matched in this PR.
Search for PRs that replace `RETURN_IF` transition logic or `#pragma dont_inline` workarounds in movement IASA functions.
